steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The sentence is: "In 1989, Missouri [blank] official fossil: the Eperisocrinus..." Two things need to be correct: (1) the subject of the verb "designated" must be plural (the legislature, i.e., lawmakers as a group), so it should be the plain noun "lawmakers" not the possessive "lawmaker's"; (2) the fossil belongs to the state, so "state" needs a possessive apostrophe: "state's."

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — lawmaker's designated the states: "lawmaker's" is a singular possessive, not a subject noun, and "states" (plural) wrongly implies multiple states own the fossil. ✗
  • Choice B — lawmakers designated the state's: Plural nominative "lawmakers" works as the subject, and "state's" correctly shows Missouri owns the fossil. ✓
  • Choice C — lawmaker's designated the state's: "lawmaker's" is a possessive, not a valid subject for "designated." ✗
  • Choice D — lawmakers designated the states: "states" is a plain plural, so there's no possessive relationship — the sentence doesn't say the fossil belongs to Missouri. ✗
That matches Choice B — lawmakers designated the state's.
